title: One mouse, not one snake
summary: A boy walks into a Copenhagen pet shop and asks for a small green snake. The owner asks four small questions before she sells anything.
body:
Mrs Linde has a small pet shop in Copenhagen. The shop is between a bookstore and a flower shop. The shop sells fish, two kinds of bird, six kinds of mouse, three small snakes, and one old parrot that does not like new people.

A boy comes in one Saturday. He is eight years old. His mother stays at the door. The boy walks past the fish. He walks past the mice. He stops at the snakes.

'I want this one,' the boy says. He points to a small green snake.

Mrs Linde sits down on her chair. She is sixty-one years old. She has sold many snakes. But she does not sell a snake the first time a child asks.

'Why this one?' she says.

'Because it is small. And green is my favourite colour.'

'Will it stay small?'

'I do not know.'

'No. It will grow. It will be three times as long in one year. Are you ready to feed a long snake?'

The boy thinks. He looks at his mother. His mother does not move.

'What do you feed it?' the boy asks.

'Mice. Live mice.'

The boy is quiet for a long time. Then he says, 'I do not want the snake. I will buy one mouse.'

Mrs Linde smiles. She says, 'That is a wise choice.'

annotations.meaningVi: chuột sống (còn sống, không phải chết)
im lặng trong một thời gian dài
lựa chọn khôn ngoan (cụm danh từ)

annotations.phrase: the first time a child asks
annotations.meaningVi: lần đầu tiên một đứa trẻ hỏi

grammarNotes.title: 'Will' for future predictions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A1 dùng 'will + động từ nguyên thể' để dự đoán điều sẽ xảy ra. Trong bài: 'It will grow.' 'It will be three times as long in one year.' 'I will buy one mouse.' Câu hỏi: 'Will it stay small?' 'Will' không thay đổi theo chủ ngữ — dùng cho mọi ngôi: I will, he will, they will. Phủ định: 'will not' hoặc 'won't'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + base verb' to predict what will happen. The passage: 'It will grow.' 'It will be three times as long in one year.' 'I will buy one mouse.' Question: 'Will it stay small?' 'Will' is the same with every subject. Negative: 'will not' or 'won't'.

translationVi:
Bà Linde có một cửa hàng thú cảnh nhỏ ở Copenhagen. Cửa hàng nằm giữa một hiệu sách và một tiệm hoa. Cửa hàng bán cá, hai loại chim, sáu loại chuột, ba con rắn nhỏ, và một con vẹt già không thích người lạ.

Một cậu bé đi vào một sáng thứ Bảy. Cậu tám tuổi. Mẹ cậu đứng ở cửa. Cậu đi qua bể cá. Cậu đi qua chuột. Cậu dừng ở rắn.

'Cháu muốn con này,' cậu bé nói. Cậu chỉ vào một con rắn xanh nhỏ.

Bà Linde ngồi xuống ghế. Bà sáu mươi mốt tuổi. Bà từng bán nhiều con rắn. Nhưng bà không bán rắn ngay lần đầu một đứa trẻ hỏi.

'Sao lại con này?' bà hỏi.

'Vì nó nhỏ. Và xanh là màu yêu thích của cháu.'

'Nó sẽ nhỏ mãi không?'

'Cháu không biết.'

'Không. Nó sẽ lớn. Sau một năm sẽ dài gấp ba. Cháu sẵn sàng cho một con rắn dài ăn chưa?'

Cậu bé suy nghĩ. Cậu nhìn mẹ. Mẹ cậu không động đậy.

'Cháu cho nó ăn gì ạ?' cậu hỏi.

'Chuột. Chuột sống.'

Cậu bé im lặng một lúc lâu. Rồi cậu nói: 'Cháu không muốn rắn nữa. Cháu mua một con chuột.'

Bà Linde mỉm cười. Bà nói: 'Đó là một lựa chọn khôn ngoan.'

options: In Copenhagen, between a bookstore and a flower shop
In London, next to a train station
questions.explanation: 'A small pet shop in Copenhagen… between a bookstore and a flower shop.'

questions.prompt: Which animal at the shop does not like new people?
questions.explanation: 'One old parrot that does not like new people.'

questions.prompt: Which snake does the boy want?
questions.explanation: 'He points to a small green snake.'

questions.prompt: Why does the boy want this snake, at first?
options: It is small, and green is his favourite colour.
questions.explanation: 'Because it is small. And green is my favourite colour.'

options: No — it will grow. In one year it will be three times as long.
Yes — it will stay this size.
questions.explanation: 'No. It will grow. It will be three times as long in one year.'

options: He does not want the snake; he will buy one mouse.
questions.explanation: 'I do not want the snake. I will buy one mouse.'

questions.prompt: How does Mrs Linde respond to his decision?
options: She smiles and calls it a wise choice.
She gives him the snake anyway.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A shopkeeper can use small honest questions to help a customer make a better choice than the first one.
Children should not visit pet shops.
Green snakes are the most popular.
questions.explanation: Mrs Linde does not lecture; she asks four questions and lets the boy decide.
questions.hint: Think about what Mrs Linde does instead of giving advice.

cloze.template:

A boy comes in one Saturday. He is {{b3}} years old. His mother stays at the door. The boy walks past the fish. He walks past the mice. He stops at the snakes.

'I want this one,' the boy says. He points to a small {{b4}} snake.

Mrs Linde sits down on her chair. She is sixty-one years old. She has sold many snakes. But she does not sell a snake the first time a child asks.

'Why this one?' she says.

'Because it is small. And green is my {{b5}} colour.'

'Will it stay small?'

'I do not know.'

'No. It will grow. It will be three times as long in one {{b6}}. Are you ready to feed a long snake?'

The boy thinks. He looks at his mother. His mother does not move.

'What do you {{b7}} it?' the boy asks.

'Mice. {{b8}} mice.'

The boy is quiet for a long time. Then he says, 'I do not want the snake. I will buy one {{b9}}.'

Mrs Linde smiles. She says, 'That is a {{b10}} choice.'

blanks.explanation: Three times as long in one year.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs Linde does not refuse the snake — she asks four small questions and lets the boy refuse it himself. Is there a time you wanted something quickly, and someone you trusted asked one careful question that changed your mind? What was the question?
